audits.excerpt: "Hard crust snapped under boots. Every drift hid stone, old ice, or a hollow deep enough to cost the line precious heartbeats... Cold bit through wool, fur, and pride alike."
audits.reason: The scene is doing real travel work, but "pride alike" turns the cold back into writerly attitude language. The ground is already there; the last beat should stay in joints, breath, or numb fingers instead of abstract character virtue.
audits.suggestedFix: Keep the snapped crust and bad footing, but replace the final clause with a bodily cost: cold through wool and fur until jaw hinges or finger joints start to ache.

audits.excerpt: "The spoor kinks there. The pull stops smearing across the flats and starts dragging everything into one place." / "Fixed sat worse than anchored. More trap in it."
audits.reason: The visible kinked spoor is good, then the passage slips back into private tracking shorthand and italicized conceptual labels. That blurs the threshold from readable ground into abstract map-room language.
audits.suggestedFix: Keep the kink in the boot spoor and the lee behind the drift, but replace the abstract follow-up with a tactical read Sylvanas could price: whatever is ahead has stopped spreading and started holding one piece of ground.

audits.excerpt: "Every time the plague-noise swallows it, it hooks the same way underneath. Same catch. Same hand on it."
audits.reason: This is still systems-talk in the middle of a materially grounded briefing. "Plague-noise" and "it" make Jaina's read feel private and schematic instead of tied to the cup, wet tabletop, mouth-taste, or hand motion already on the page.
audits.suggestedFix: Keep her fingertip in the spilled ale, but make the comparison cash out physically: when the sword cuts across the same taste or catch she knows from the plague, it hooks underneath in the same place.

audits.excerpt: "The pressure in the wards grew until it resolved into one instruction: leave. Turn back."
audits.reason: This reintroduces internal system language right before the cave threshold, where the chapter is strongest when it stays in teeth, knuckles, leather, and the body's refusal. The command itself is good; the "pressure in the wards" wrapper is the float.
audits.suggestedFix: Strip the ward-summary phrasing and let the warning arrive through the body: the ache behind her teeth sharpens, bow leather hardens, skin tightens, and the instinct to turn back lands before thought does.

audits.excerpt: "They're standing where the blade catches it," Jaina said, too fast. "Don't let them bracket us. And don't cast straight through the middle unless I tell you. The cave will kick it sideways."
audits.reason: The back half is better, but "where the blade catches it" still leaves the warning contactless. In first contact, Jaina needs to name what dies there — a cast, frost, force — so the room's behavior stays actionable instead of schematic.
audits.suggestedFix: Keep the bracket warning and the cave kicking the cast sideways, but replace the vague first clause with something explicit: they're standing where the blade catches a spell before it reaches Arthas.

audits.excerpt: "Whatever had stood in that chamber wearing Arthas's face had left the prince behind with the hammer in the snow."
audits.reason: This lands as a strong thesis statement, but it floats above the physical evidence at the exact moment the retreat should stay with arrow failure, frozen blood, and the abandoned hammer. The beat needs colder field-reading, not identity-poetry.
audits.suggestedFix: Keep the arrow memory and Muradin's blood, then replace the sentence with an external verdict Sylvanas can actually price: the man in the cave took the shot, left Light's Vengeance in the snow, and watched them run without following.

audits.excerpt: "No despair in that. Jaina did not run that way."
audits.reason: The carry-out beat is strong when it lives in Jaina's bare shaking hands. This sentence pulls the moment back into authorial character summary instead of leaving the proof in the repeated hooked motion and her ragged speech.
audits.suggestedFix: Cut the summary sentence and let the evidence do the work: keep her fingers jumping back to the same hooked turn while she walks and answers.
